title: podman not working on WSL (Docker Desktop/Podman Desktop on Windows)
body:
### Issue Description

When I do podman info/podman build on Oracle Linux 10/9, AlmaLinux 10, Ubuntu I get a filesystem related error message

Preivously podman was working fine, have used it extensively on oraclelinux:9

Error appears when running podman info/build with containers running within both Podman (Podman Desktop) and Docker (Docker Desktop)

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Run oraclelinux:10, almalinux:10 or ubuntu:latest from docker hub via` docker run -it --privileged container-name /bin/bash`, with or without --privileged result is the same
2. Install podman - `dnf install -y podman` or `apt-get update && apt-get install podman`
3. Run `podman info` or `podman build .` with a generic Containerfile


### Describe the results you received

`podman info` and `podman build .` return this and nothing else:

Container tag in Docker Hub - **almalinux:10**

```
WARN[0000] "/" is not a shared mount, this could cause issues or missing mounts with rootless containers
cannot clone: Operation not permitted
Error: cannot re-exec process
```

Container tag in Docker Hub - **ubuntu:latest**

```
WARN[0000] "/" is not a shared mount, this could cause issues or missing mounts with rootless containers
cannot clone: Operation not permitted
Error: cannot re-exec process
```

Container tag in Docker Hub - **oraclelinux:10**

`Error: configure storage: 'overlay' is not supported over overlayfs, a mount_program is required: backing file system is unsupported for this graph driver`

### Describe the results you expected

podman info - list podman information
podman build - start linting the Containerfile, pulling base image, building the image, etc

### podman info output


**almalinux:10 - 6:5.4.0-12.el10_0 @appstream**

```yaml
WARN[0000] "/" is not a shared mount, this could cause issues or missing mounts with rootless containers
cannot clone: Operation not permitted
Error: cannot re-exec process
```

**ubuntu:latest - 4.9.3+ds1-1ubuntu0.2**

```yaml
WARN[0000] "/" is not a shared mount, this could cause issues or missing mounts with rootless containers
cannot clone: Operation not permitted
Error: cannot re-exec process
```

**oraclelinux:10 - 6:5.4.0-12.0.1.el10_0 @ol10_appstream**

```yaml
Error: configure storage: 'overlay' is not supported over overlayfs, a mount_program is required: backing file system is unsupported for this graph driver
```

### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

**System Information:**

```
Processor	13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-13950HX (2.20 GHz)
Installed RAM	64.0 GB (63.7 GB usable)
System type	64-bit operating system, x64-based processor
```

**Windows Information:**

```
Edition	Windows 11 Home
Version	24H2
Installed on	‎5/‎8/‎2025
OS build	26100.4652
Experience	Windows Feature Experience Pack 1000.26100.128.0
```

**wsl --version**

```
WSL version: 2.5.9.0
Kernel version: 6.6.87.2-1
WSLg version: 1.0.66
MSRDC version: 1.2.6074
Direct3D version: 1.611.1-81528511
DXCore version: 10.0.26100.1-240331-1435.ge-release
Windows version: 10.0.26100.4652
```

**docker --version**

```
Docker version 28.3.2, build 578ccf6
```

Docker Desktop 4.43.2 (up to date)

**podman --version**

```
podman version 5.5.2
```

Podman Desktop v1.20.2 (up to date)

Podman Machine - Podman v5.5.2

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Hi @emanuilov 

This is a common issue when using Podman in Podman. The first Podman container needs to be launched with enough permissions to launch another container inside it. These extra permissions are not enabled by default as by default, the container are locked down for security. 

You can see many issues like yours which I will list below but the errors I'm looking at are:

Lack of permissions:
```
cannot clone: Operation not permitted
Error: cannot re-exec process
```

Trying to use a nested overlayfs, this is not supported. The first Podman container needs to be launched using something other than an overlayfs backing it. 
```
Error: configure storage: 'overlay' is not supported over overlayfs, a mount_program is required: backing file system is unsupported for this graph driver
```

Further more if you are using Docker as the first container layer, extra permissions are needed for that as well: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/24512

Maybe this link can help you: https://www.redhat.com/en/blog/podman-inside-container

https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/22606

Hi @ninja-quokka ,
My mistake, so it seems with --privileged ubuntu:latest and oraclelinux:9, almalinux:9 and redhat/ubi9 works fine on Windows, however on OracleLinux 10, AlmaLinux 10 and redhat/ubi10, I'm still getting the same error - Error: configure storage: 'overlay' is not supported over overlayfs, a mount_program is required: backing file system is unsupported for this graph driver

I assume something changed with RedHat 10s release that has changed podman's behavior when checking for compatibility when running within a container on WSL/Windows

To reproduce you can try out these commands on a Windows host:

```
docker run -it --privileged image-name /bin/bash

dnf install -y podman
podman info
```

When I change the image to oraclelinux:10 or almalinux:10 or redhat/ubi10 I get the overlayfs error.

@ninja-quokka Ultimately it'd be best if this change in behaviour, between RedHat 9 and 10 containers running on Windows WSL, is documented/fixed. Do let me know if this gets fixed eventually please.

In the meantime here's a simpler workaround for Oracle Linux 10:
1. Create a file - /etc/containers/storage.conf
2. Add this in it, where /build-tmp is a Docker volume:
```
[storage]
driver = "overlay"

runroot = "/build-tmp"

graphroot = "/build-tmp"
```
 
According to the defaut storage.conf file, these are also supported dirs, the /usr one didn't work on OL10, haven't tried the rest:
```
#  Note: The storage.conf file overrides other storage.conf files based on this precedence:
#      /usr/containers/storage.conf
#      /etc/containers/storage.conf
#      $HOME/.config/containers/storage.conf
#      $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/containers/storage.conf (if XDG_CONFIG_HOME is set)
```

After applying this if getting errors such as `internal:0:0-0: Error: Could not process rule: No such file or director`, follow [this ](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/25201) issue

vi /etc/containers/containers.conf
[engine]
cgroup_manager = "cgroupfs"
我是这样修改就可以了。

我还添加了下面的配置
[network]
network_backend = "cni"
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
